hooks.prompt: If this Bash command is a git commit, ensure the commit message follows Conventional Commits format: type(optional scope): description. Allowed types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert. If the message does not follow this format, block the tool call and suggest a corrected message. If it already follows the format, approve silently.

Check if the file path being written contains a type-based directory inside a features/ directory. Type-based directories are: hooks/, components/, schemas/, presenters/. For example: features/items/hooks/use-items.ts or features/items/components/item-card.tsx.

If the path matches this pattern (features/*/hooks/, features/*/components/, features/*/schemas/, features/*/presenters/), BLOCK the tool call and explain:

"BLOCKED: Do not create type-based directories inside features. Use concern-based colocation instead:
- API hooks (.queries.ts, .mutations.ts) go in features/<domain>/api/ next to the .api.ts file
- Form files (.schema.ts, .controller.ts, .view.tsx) go in features/<domain>/<concern>/
- Feature files (.controller.ts, .presenter.ts, .view.tsx) go in features/<domain>/<concern>/
- Shared components within the feature go flat at features/<domain>/"

If the path does NOT match (e.g., features/items/api/items.queries.ts, features/items/create-item/item-form.view.tsx, shared/components/), approve silently.
